snippets.title: Building and inspecting paths
snippets.code:
from pathlib import Path

p = Path("data") / "prompts" / "system.txt"
print(p)          # data/prompts/system.txt (or data\\prompts\\system.txt on Windows)
print(p.name)     # system.txt
print(p.stem)     # system
print(p.suffix)   # .txt
print(p.parent)   # data/prompts
snippets.explanation: The / operator builds OS-correct paths; the path properties extract parts without string parsing.

snippets.title: Read, write and create directories
snippets.code:
from pathlib import Path

out = Path(".cache/embeddings")
out.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)   # create nested dirs, no error if present

(out / "note.txt").write_text("cached\n")
if (out / "note.txt").exists():
    print((out / "note.txt").read_text())   # cached
snippets.explanation: pathlib reads/writes text in one call and creates directory trees safely.

snippets.title: Glob a document tree for ingestion
snippets.code:
from pathlib import Path

docs = Path("docs")
for md in docs.rglob("*.md"):     # recursive
    text = md.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    print(md, len(text))
snippets.explanation: rglob walks subdirectories; glob matches a single level. Each match is a Path ready to read.

snippets.title: Read config from environment variables
snippets.code:
import os

api_key = os.environ.get("OPENAI_API_KEY")
if not api_key:
    raise RuntimeError("set OPENAI_API_KEY in your environment")

model = os.environ.get("MODEL", "gpt-4o")   # default if unset
print(model)
snippets.explanation: os.environ.get reads env vars with an optional default, keeping secrets out of source code.
